verbGlossary.ABSORBS: {source} provides a monetary cushion that absorbs cost impact when {target} materializes, preventing budget overrun (e.g., allowance absorbs design-risk scope gaps)
verbGlossary.APPORTIONS: {source} structure distributes {target} proportionally across line items or parties (e.g., SOV apportions risk allocation across work packages)
verbGlossary.ASSIGNED TO: {source} line item is linked to a responsible party who owns the associated {target} (e.g., CCO line item assigned to risk owner for backcharge tracking)
verbGlossary.BUFFERS AGAINST: {source} reserve provides a dedicated buffer that prevents {target} category from causing immediate project distress (e.g., contingency buffers against financial risk)
verbGlossary.CLASSIFIES: {source} classification code categorizes or tags {target} reserve for tracking purposes (e.g., budget code classifies contingency reserve by risk type)
verbGlossary.CONSTRAINED BY: {source} projection is limited or shaped by {target} factor that restricts cash timing or availability (e.g., cash flow projection constrained by schedule risk)
verbGlossary.CONTRIBUTES TO: {source} line item adds to the cumulative monetary {target} for a given risk (e.g., commitment line item contributes to overall risk exposure value)
verbGlossary.DEPLETES: Adverse financial trend draws down {target} reserve over time (e.g., cost growth depletes contingency reserve)
verbGlossary.ENACTS: {source} action formally executes {target} response strategy (e.g., budget modification enacts a risk response by reallocating funds)
verbGlossary.ESCALATES: {source} event increases the severity or urgency of {target} (e.g., change event escalates financial risk when costs exceed thresholds)
verbGlossary.EVALUATED BY: {source} item is assessed through {target} evaluation process before approval (e.g., potential change order evaluated by impact assessment)
verbGlossary.EXPOSES TO: {source} or transaction creates vulnerability to {target} category (e.g., purchase order exposes project to supply chain risk)
verbGlossary.FEEDS: {source} provides input data to {target} assessment framework (e.g., CPI feeds risk matrix scoring)
verbGlossary.FUNDS: {source} provides the monetary backing for {target} reserve (e.g., contingency funds the contingency reserve)
verbGlossary.HELD AGAINST: {source} amount is withheld as security against {target} until obligations are fulfilled (e.g., retainage held against residual risk of incomplete work)
verbGlossary.INCORPORATES: {source} plan formally includes {target}s as part of its structure (e.g., project budget incorporates risk register items as line items)
verbGlossary.INFORMS: {source} data provides decision-making input to {target} assessment (e.g., cost forecast informs likelihood assessment of overrun)
verbGlossary.LINKED TO: {source} classification element is associated with {target} tracking structure for traceability (e.g., WBS code linked to risk register entries)
verbGlossary.MAPPED TO: {source} categorization corresponds to {target} classification for cross-domain analysis (e.g., cost code mapped to risk category)
verbGlossary.MIRRORS: {source} and {target} track the same underlying phenomenon from different perspectives (e.g., change order rate mirrors risk change order rate)
verbGlossary.MITIGATES: {source} mechanism reduces the probability or impact of {target} (e.g., retainage mitigates construction execution risk by ensuring completion)
verbGlossary.QUANTIFIES: {source} data assigns a dollar value to {target} or its impact (e.g., change event line item quantifies impact assessment in dollars)
verbGlossary.REALLOCATES: {source} change redistributes cost responsibility among parties in response to {target} event (e.g., commitment change order reallocates risk between GC and sub)
verbGlossary.RESERVED AGAINST: {source} funds are set aside specifically to cover a named {target} if it occurs (e.g., contingency reserved against geotechnical risk)
verbGlossary.RESPONDS TO: {source} action is taken as a direct reaction to {target} that has materialized (e.g., budget modification responds to residual risk)
verbGlossary.SHIFTS: {source} moves {target} ownership from one party to another (e.g., owner change order shifts risk allocation to the owner)
verbGlossary.SIGNALS: {source} serves as an early warning indicator that {target} is emerging or worsening (e.g., budget variance signals financial risk)
verbGlossary.TRANSFERS: {source} commitment moves {target} responsibility to another party through contractual obligation (e.g., subcontract transfers construction execution risk to subcontractor)
verbGlossary.TRANSFERS VIA: {source} serves as the contractual vehicle through which {target} is formally allocated to another party (e.g., subcontract transfers risk via risk allocation clause)
verbGlossary.TRIGGERED BY: {source} event or instrument is caused or initiated by {target} materializing (e.g., change event triggered by design risk when unforeseen conditions require scope changes)
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: {source} event or threshold crossing activates {target} response or escalation (e.g., payment delay triggers risk trigger for subcontractor default)
